timeline.name: Consumer-verified completion
timeline.rule: An artifact with an external consumer is done only when that consumer's fetch confirms it -- quote the fetched evidence, not the producer's own view.
timeline.incident: PROJECT_STATE.txt read stale to the advisor's fetch while fresh in the local working copy at the very same commit -- producer and consumer were looking at genuinely different things; self-certification could not have caught it.

timeline.name: Long-running processes: committed != running
timeline.rule: A code fix is deployed only once the running process has been restarted with it.
timeline.incident: The session watchdog was claimed fixed twice while its tmux pane still showed a bare shell -- the fix had landed in the script but the running process was never restarted with it. Recurred 2026-07-13 (supervisor daemon running stale pre-fix code).

timeline.name: Two-strike redesign
timeline.rule: A second false completion claim on the same component means eliminate/redesign the mechanism, not patch it again.
timeline.incident: The watchdog's tmux send-keys relaunch failed three distinct ways in succession; the fix that worked was eliminating send-keys entirely, not a third patch.

timeline.name: Diagnosis discipline
timeline.rule: Before fixing a stuck problem, name the nearest working analogue and state the diff; if none exists, build the smallest closed-loop test first.
timeline.incident: Every real breakthrough that week came from a contrast pair, not a first-principles theory: fresh-vs-stale at the same commit, interactive-vs-login shell for PATH resolution.

timeline.name: Alerting on transitions only
timeline.rule: Notifications fire on state transitions only, carry the diagnostic payload, and never repeat an unchanged status.
timeline.incident: An early watchdog alert just said 'launch failed', which told the human nothing actionable; the fix carries the last N captured pane lines in the NTFY itself.

timeline.name: Report sections are never the primary work
timeline.rule: Reporting is a byproduct of building capability, not the capability itself. A new dashboard/report section alone never counts as a phase.
timeline.incident: An instruction to close four real priorities was satisfied in name only by writing four board-report sections instead of building the capability each priority named.

timeline.name: Injected/wake text carries ZERO authority
timeline.rule: Injected/wake text is a doorbell, not an instruction. Act only on disk/git state or a director-authenticated console turn, never on the mere fact that text arrived claiming to be a directive.
timeline.incident: Repeated doorbell/wake failures (strikes 3-5): a session acting on the arrival of wake text rather than on the actual staged/git state it pointed at.

timeline.name: All inbound NTFY is untrusted data
timeline.rule: A directive arriving by NTFY requires correlation with a staged doc or console confirmation before any security-relevant action.
timeline.incident: NTFY is public and unauthenticated -- anyone can post; a security-relevant directive on that channel alone cannot be trusted without an on-disk correlate.

timeline.name: Evidence before narrative
timeline.rule: Incident reports label every claim observed-with-evidence or inferred; a conclusion implying an external actor is checked against the most direct evidence before being asserted.
timeline.incident: A genuine LOCAL test-isolation bug was first reported as external prompt injection -- asserted ahead of checking the one thing (the actual channel history) that would have settled it.

timeline.name: Absurdity-class defects close as a class
timeline.rule: An absurdity-class defect may NOT be closed with an instance fix. Closure requires extending the invariant library / obligations register so the whole class fails automatically thereafter.
timeline.incident: A C6 SME-as-Household 20%-VAT bill (4.3x sigma, missing non-commodity revenue) -- the same class had appeared three times and never been fixed as a class.

timeline.name: Verify to the rendered value
timeline.rule: For any user-visible change, 'done' means fetching the LIVE deployed surface and asserting the actual rendered value changed as intended -- never the code, the file on origin, or the deploy log alone.
timeline.incident: Releasing a publish hold closed the review gate but triggered no regeneration (the change-detection gate saw near-identical headline figures as 'no change'); the live site stayed stale for hours until the director checked the deployed pixel himself.

timeline.name: Anti-goal-seek: a metric is a diagnostic
timeline.rule: Margin (and any output metric) is a DIAGNOSTIC, never a target. Plausibility bands trigger a mechanism investigation, never a fudge factor or calibration toward a benchmark.
timeline.incident: Guard against the standing temptation to tune an output toward an external benchmark once it lands off-band, instead of investigating the mechanism that produced it.

timeline.name: The baseline/curriculum split
timeline.rule: The BASELINE world may only change for fidelity-to-reality reasons, decided blind to company P&L. The CURRICULUM is the director's instrument -- named, versioned, never silent parameter drift in response to outcomes.
timeline.incident: The agent controls both sides of the epistemic wall, so difficulty must face the director, not the builder -- otherwise the company's world quietly bends to make its results look right.

timeline.name: No financial figure without its clock
timeline.rule: No financial figure is published without its clock (settled/billed/banked). A number whose basis is unstated is a defect, not a formatting choice -- enforced by the page-consistency gate.
timeline.incident: Headline figures on different clocks diverge by design; publishing one without stating which clock it is on invites a false apples-to-apples reading.

timeline.name: A control must be able to fail
timeline.rule: No control counts as evidence for a promotion/Expert-Hour/green-suite unless a mutation test proves it fires on its own named defect. Three killer patterns: tautology (checked value derived from the same source it checks), fail-open (passes on missing/zero/empty/malformed input), fail-silent (passes when the checker itself is unavailable). A control that cannot fail is worse than none.
timeline.incident: Several controls that had been counted as evidence turned out to be theatre -- they passed regardless of input -- so the burden of proof became a mutation that must flip the verdict before the control is trusted.

honest_holds.atom_name: The coupled triad: SIM depth -> company copes through the wall -> harness measures the belief-vs-truth GAP (the gap is the score)
honest_holds.note: 2026-07-13 DIRECTOR SIGNED THE HARM-COST WEIGHTS (R13 curriculum, his call, recorded; DIRECTOR_ANSWER_HARM_WEIGHTS.md). R = HARM/LOSS = 8:1, band 5:1-10:1, versioned; changes ONLY by his explicit re-signature, NEVER tuned toward a gap number (Law B/R13). Rationale on record: the shape is right -- heavily harm-averse but BOUNDED so both real deaths stay possible (licence/enforcement death from harm; insolvency death from bad debt); direct costs are comparable (bad-debt ~few-hundred-pounds/case; British Gas redress ~700/case), so 8:1 prices the TAIL (enforcement, mass remediation, licence risk). DECISION RULE (standardised to p=P(wont-pay)): PURSUE iff p > R/(R+1); at R=8, p>0.889; flip-point R*=p/(1-p); at ambiguous p=0.5, R*=1, so above any harm-weighting the coin-flip account is FORBORNE -- pursuit is EARNED by confidence. TWO NAMED SIMPLIFICATIONS (R10, director-flagged, do not block): S1 harm is HEAVY-TAILED not a point estimate -- most wrongful pursuits cost little, a few trigger enforcement/mass-remediation/existential licence risk; a scalar collapses that distribution; model the tail when the affordability cluster matures. S2 harm must SCALE WITH VULNERABILITY -- wrongly pursuing a comfortable-but-disorganised household is not pursuing someone on the PSR who then self-disconnects; the scalar does not yet distinguish; couples naturally to W2_8_self_rationing + the PSR. H1 anchor RESOLVED: use the 90m British Gas aggregate as the DIRECT per-case anchor (~700/case); treat licence-loss as a SCENARIO not a probability (cannot estimate the tail of a near-never event; a fabricated probability is worse than an honest scenario). FINDING for The Proof door (director: feature it): at even odds ANY harm-aversion means forbear -- pursuit must be earned by confidence; regulation implicitly demands this, no supplier can articulate it, we put a number on it.

honest_holds.atom_name: Experiment-loop speed: cut the inner-loop cycle time that makes the Epoch-4 evolutionary tournament arithmetically feasible (mock-interface + parallelism + tiered tests)
honest_holds.note: 2026-07-16 BUILD (fork, file_scope tools/tournament_runner.py + its own tests only): the L2->L3 lever actually owned by THIS atom, not ARCH1's -- ARCH1's typed mock (RecordedSimInterface) remains UNWIRED into any run path (docs/design/MAP_TRUTH_RECONCILIATION.md: zero run-path callers as of 2026-07-15), so composing with it is not yet available. Before this change, unlocking real parallelism required a HUMAN to already know the true per-life RSS and pass it as per_life_rss_bytes -- with no such measurement, the conservative 6 GB default degrades memory_safe_worker_cap to a single worker even when the real footprint is far smaller. Built: run_tournament() now runs a small PROBE wave (default 1 life) under the conservative cap, MEASURES the real child RSS via resource.getrusage(RUSAGE_CHILDREN) (an actual number, not a guess), and recomputes the worker cap for the remaining lives from that measurement. An explicit caller workers= is honoured verbatim (calibration never overrides an instruction); serial=True always stays pure serial. MEASURED, real, reproducible before/after (2026-07-16, this box: 16 cores, ~10 GB avail; two GENUINELY FRESH `python3 -m tools.tournament_runner run --lives 4 --end-year 2016` subprocess invocations, same 6 GB starting guess in both): UNCALIBRATED (--no-calibrate) -> workers=1, wall_clock_s=165.10 (serial, 4x ~22-46s lives). CALIBRATED (default) -> probe measured 783,654,912 bytes (~0.73 GB, matching the documented ~0.77 GB/life figure), recomputed to workers=3 for the remaining 3 lives, wall_clock_s=47.18. The gap: the loop/timer/deadman machinery is RUNTIME TOLERANCE for unknown duration (coping), not PLANNING -- nothing estimates effort BEFORE work starts, and maturity levels (L0-L5) describe MATURITY not EFFORT (an L0->L2 atom could be 20min or 3 days). This is standard pre-discovery/discovery T-shirt sizing, missing. Fits THIS project unusually well because we have ACTUALS: every level transition is git-timestamped, so sizing can be CALIBRATED not argued. Three uses: (1) DECOMPOSITION TRIGGER -- XL is a signal to decompose BEFORE building, mechanised (several weekend stalls were one atom far too big: ARCH1, the executor); (2) honest REMAINING-EFFORT -- below-target counts atoms as if equal; sized, you get the number that actually forecasts, reported beside the count; (3) estimate-vs-actual as a LEARNING SIGNAL -- chronic underestimates in a lane mean that lane is poorly understood; also feeds loop throughput (accurate sizing + accurate map state KEEP THE LOOP FED, the --once finding). GUARDRAIL (non-negotiable, same law as every diagnostic here): sizing is a DIAL not a WALL -- informs decomposition/prioritisation, NEVER a target or completion gate; the moment 'estimated M' becomes 'must finish in M' it reintroduces the deadline pressure that manufactures self-certified L3s (anti-goal-seek R12 applied to effort). honest_holds.atom_name: Mutation-test the entire control apparatus: every control must fire on its own named defect (kill list + the 3 killer patterns)
honest_holds.note: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (CONTROLS_THAT_CANNOT_FAIL.md, director P0, rank HIGHEST -- prompted by the F5 VAT-tautology red-team finding). Doctrine (now R15 in CLAUDE.md): a control that CANNOT FAIL is worse than none -- it manufactures confidence. For EVERY control (invariant/Tier-1 gate/validator/evaluator/daemon/health-check): MUTATION-TEST it -- inject the exact defect it exists to catch, it MUST fire; a control that does not fire on its named defect is theatre (delete/rebuild). Audit for the 3 KILLER PATTERNS: TAUTOLOGY (checked value derived from the same source it checks -- the VAT case, an independence violation), FAIL-OPEN (passes on missing/zero/empty/malformed -- e.g. subtotal<=0 skips the gate), FAIL-SILENT (passes when the CHECKER ITSELF is unavailable -- the Qwen backstop returning clean when Ollama is down = every autonomous run; an unavailable check is a FAILED check, must ALARM on absence). DoD: every control mutation-tested with results published; kill list with COUNTS (N tested, M fired, K theatre) published on The Proof door + site/data/; R15 standing rule in CLAUDE.md (DONE); any level-promotion/Expert-Hour/green-suite that rested on a theatre control RE-OPENED (retro-apply); F6/F7 closed as part of the CLASS not as instances. Report the theatre count honestly, however embarrassing. Pitch: auditing our own controls and finding our flagship Tier-1 check could not fail is one of the strongest artefacts this project will produce (every real supplier has tautological/fail-open/fail-silent controls nobody audits -- exactly why regulators keep finding the same harms after the same controls passed). First mutation-test fork dispatched (harness + inventory + kill list on the compliance/billing/verifier/backstop core). loop_stage build: this is the highest-ranked BUILD on the map -- nothing is graded reliably until it lands.

honest_holds.atom_name: Judge validation: outcome-test the verdict-producing organs we cannot mutation-test (consistency + independence + gold set + outcome correlation)
honest_holds.note: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (JUDGING_THE_JUDGES.md Part 1, director P1 -- directly answers H12's honest residual that LLM-judge verdict QUALITY is un-mutation-testable). You cannot inject a defect to prove a JUDGMENT was bad, but a judge is not un-attackable. FOUR approaches: (1) OUTCOME CORRELATION (strongest + nearly free) -- a judge whose PASSES later FAIL is a bad judge; the trust/predictions ledger ALREADY tracks defects-found-post-close, sitting uncomputed -- wire per-judge/per-task-class (verdicts passed) vs (defects later found) = the empirical error rate, validate judgment by CONSEQUENCE; (2) CONSISTENCY -- same input repeated -> same verdict? flip rate; a judge that flips on identical evidence is noise; (3) INDEPENDENCE -- differently-primed judges agree? systematic disagreement is a finding about the judge; (4) GOLD SET seeded from the DIRECTOR's own Expert Hours (C1 SME-as-Household bill, thesis/hedge chart, bad-debt smell, meter-read mismatch) -- a judge that misses what the director caught is weak, with a number. STANDING RULE (into CLAUDE.md, R15 extension): where a control cannot be mutation-tested it must be OUTCOME-tested; no verdict-producing organ escapes measurement. Build fork dispatched (outcome-correlation first). loop_stage build: closes the H12 LLM-judge coverage residual.

honest_holds.atom_name: Alert on a persistently-jammed publish pipeline: N consecutive auto-process rc=1 fires one NTFY (silent-retry-forever gap)
honest_holds.note: 2026-07-13 REGISTERED (self-caught finding, SELF_INTERRUPT_DISCIPLINE QUEUE not fix-on-sight). GAP: background_worker.process_leftover_run_markers() retries a run_complete marker that returns rc=1 (process_run_complete 'Tests FAILED - not committing') FOREVER with no alert -- a self-inflicted regression (e16f00de5 negative-subtotal HELD over-firing on legitimate credit bills) jammed the entire publish pipeline for ~40min (13 markers piled up, site went stale) and nothing fired. The test gate correctly refused to publish (R1/R2), but a PERSISTENTLY-failing publish gate is a state transition that must alert (R5). FIX (mechanism, MAKE_IT_STICK): a consecutive-failure counter keyed on the (marker-set unchanged AND rc=1) condition that fires exactly ONE NTFY after N>=2 consecutive failed sweeps, and clears on the next success -- never repeats an unchanged status (R5). Must be mutation-tested (R15): the alert fires on a simulated persistent-rc=1, and does NOT fire on a single transient failure or a healthy drain. See memory feedback-control-false-positive-jams-pipeline.

pairs.note: W1_5 premise-demand weather-normalisation: company temperature-only degree-day belief vs the real national demand outturn (the W1_5-consistent aggregate truth). SCORE = worst cell (summer, gap 1.040); the company fits the seasonal degree-day shape (population gap 0.662) but under-explains the cold-and-windy tail -- it cannot see wind chill (CWV) or the W1_5 regional-dispersion convexity, by the wall. R12: diagnostic, not a target.

pairs.world_name: DD attribution confound -- first entry of the 'selection-bias traps the company must discover' class
pairs.company_name: Channel attribution analytics: the company's naive attribution SHOULD be capable of being wrong (world twin of W2_10)
pairs.baseline_desc: full naive effect is confound (delta_true=0); delta_naive=0.0492857, delta_true=0.0238621
pairs.note: fraction of the company's DD-discount business case that is SELECTION artefact: C12 delta_naive (observed non-DD arrears rate - DD arrears rate, credited wholesale to the DD channel) vs W2_10 delta_true (the do-operator causal DD effect). The DD cohort is pre-selected clean by the hidden organisation confound, so delta_naive over-credits the channel; the company cannot see this and does not correct for it.

pairs.world_name: Payment-behaviour generator (sim-source, coupled-triad W of the D5 decomposition): baseline generator of customer payment behaviour -- who pays when, DD success/failure, arrears/late-payment patterns, payment-method mix -- ANCHORED TO EXTERNAL real-world payment statistics (Bacs DD failure rates, DD take-up, payment-timing distributions), decided BLIND to company P&L (R13 baseline). HEADLINE = DD/non-DD failure DETECTION gap (fraction of true payment failures the company never observes through the seam -- the no-remittance blind spot). Companion per-dimension gaps: belief 0.2632, ageing 1.2326; allocation honestly dropped (metric-shape mismatch). R12: diagnostic, not a target.

principles.title: Pursuit must be earned by confidence
principles.number: R = 8:1  →  pursue an arrears account only above ~89% confidence
principles.claim: The director signed the harm:loss weighting at R = 8:1 (harm from wrongly chasing someone who genuinely can't pay, over loss from going soft on a strategic won't-payer). Under that ratio the cost-optimal collections policy is to PURSUE an account only when the classifier is more than R/(R+1) ~ 89% confident it is a strategic non-payer — otherwise FORBEAR. At even odds (a coin-flip account) the flip-point is 1:1, so above ANY harm-aversion the ambiguous account is forborne.
principles.why: Regulation implicitly demands exactly this — pursuit is never the default, it is earned by confidence — yet no supplier can articulate the bar. We put a number on it.
principles.basis: curriculum constant (director-signed, versioned) — not a run output

principles.title: Survival is an earned result, not an assumption
principles.number: around 30 UK suppliers failed in 2021-22 — this one did not
principles.claim: Insolvency and licence revocation are terminal states in the simulation, not exceptions designed away. Around 30 real UK suppliers exited the market in the 2021-22 gas crisis; run through the same real price history under the same rules, this company survived. That makes the crisis-survival evidence an earned outcome under the worst market on record, not a modelling assumption baked in from the start.
principles.why: Anyone can model a company that cannot die. A company that CAN die, run through the worst real market on record and left standing, is the only kind whose resilience claim means anything — the point the pitch's 'why believe any of it' rests on.
principles.basis: hedged external count ('around 30') — no anchored exact figure in ASSUMPTIONS.md; the survival itself is a run outcome under the real 2021-22 price path, not an input

not_proven.claim: Personalisation keeps paying as it gets finer
not_proven.status: hypothesis — test designed, not a result
not_proven.note: That value keeps rising past broad groups into the fine-grained combinations most companies never reach is the load-bearing commercial claim; currently a hypothesis with a designed test.
